Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Slider-7.2+13.5*0+6.3swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

2.Kevin McGonigleLHBDET vs Mason Fluharty (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.118 / xBA 0.077 / xSLG 0.113 (-197 pts)K 26.5%BB 15.9%HR 0.4%Hit 3.2%Strong Pitcher

Kevin McGonigle LHB vs Mason Fluharty LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 779+0+0, pitcher rows 284+1017+0

Verdict

Fluharty's cutter is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.118 xwOBA (-197 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -22 pts (raw 0.168 -> adj 0.146); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -28 pts (bb_type-adj 0.146 -> final 0.118); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Kevin McGonigle is on a 14-day slump (-65 pts vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Sweeper53.10.3300.197GB 39% / LD 11% / FB 45% / PU 5%0.320-100.2020.1450.21423.5
Cutter46.90.1640.271GB 37% / LD 23% / FB 17% / PU 23%0.127-370.0830.0000.00017.8

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 68.4 mph
swing length 6.8 ft
attack angle 16.7°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Sweeper-8.0+16.7*4+8.7swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Cutter-5.2+20.97+15.6swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

3.Darell HernaizRHBATH vs Trevor McDonald (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.142 / xBA 0.190 / xSLG 0.142 (-173 pts)K 17.2%BB 1.8%HR 0.9%Hit 13.5%Strong Pitcher

Darell Hernaiz RHB vs Trevor McDonald RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 308+712+511, pitcher rows 182+213+39

Verdict

McDonald's slider is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.142 xwOBA (-173 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -41 pts (raw 0.191 -> adj 0.150); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.150 -> final 0.142); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

4.Zack GelofRHBATH vs Trevor McDonald (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.150 / xBA 0.159 / xSLG 0.139 (-165 pts)K 36.4%BB 1.7%HR 1.6%Hit 12.6%Strong Pitcher

Zack Gelof RHB vs Trevor McDonald RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 320+416+2056, pitcher rows 182+213+39

Verdict

McDonald's slider is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.150 xwOBA (-165 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -21 pts (raw 0.164 -> adj 0.143); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.143 -> final 0.150); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Zack Gelof is on a 14-day heater (+44 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

5.Esteury RuizRHBMIA vs Nick Martinez (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.159 / xBA 0.154 / xSLG 0.229 (-156 pts)K 23.4%BB 1.4%HR 2.7%Hit 11.3%Strong Pitcher

Esteury Ruiz RHB vs Nick Martinez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 99+88+246, pitcher rows 669+2603+2126

Verdict

Martinez's sinker is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.159 xwOBA (-156 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +6 pts (raw 0.153 -> adj 0.159); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

6.Ryan KreidlerRHBMIN vs Logan Henderson (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.163 / xBA 0.169 / xSLG 0.218 (-152 pts)K 61.3%BB 4.3%HR 0.4%Hit 14.6%Strong Pitcher

Ryan Kreidler RHB vs Logan Henderson RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 105+179+263, pitcher rows 185+410+0

Verdict

Henderson's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Ryan Kreidler should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.163 xwOBA (-152 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -63 pts (bb_type-adj 0.226 -> final 0.163); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Henry Bolte RHB vs Trevor McDonald RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 39+0+0, pitcher rows 182+213+39

Verdict

McDonald's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Henry Bolte should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.167 xwOBA (-148 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -46 pts (raw 0.213 -> adj 0.167); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

8.Drew RomoLHBCWS vs Jameson Taillon (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.169 / xBA 0.181 / xSLG 0.424 (-146 pts)K 22.7%BB 10.3%HR 3.2%Hit 8.1%Strong Pitcher

Drew Romo LHB vs Jameson Taillon RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 164+12+189, pitcher rows 744+2114+2483

Verdict

Taillon's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Drew Romo should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.169 xwOBA (-146 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +8 pts (raw 0.286 -> adj 0.293); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -124 pts (bb_type-adj 0.293 -> final 0.169); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Drew Romo is on a 14-day slump (-77 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

9.MJ MelendezLHBNYM vs Carlos Rodón (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.175 / xBA 0.177 / xSLG 0.352 (-140 pts)K 29.5%BB 2.2%HR 2.9%Hit 8.5%Strong Pitcher

MJ Melendez LHB vs Carlos Rodón LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 271+255+1919, pitcher rows 78+3375+3415

Verdict

Rodón's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while MJ Melendez should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.175 xwOBA (-140 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -7 pts (raw 0.256 -> adj 0.250); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -75 pts (bb_type-adj 0.250 -> final 0.175); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Slider38.90.2870.195GB 44% / LD 19% / FB 32% / PU 4%0.285-30.1640.1530.28034.4

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

10.Jose TrevinoRHBCIN vs Joey Cantillo (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.185 / xBA 0.183 / xSLG 0.278 (-130 pts)K 14.8%BB 7.8%HR 0.9%Hit 12.0%Strong Pitcher

Jose Trevino RHB vs Joey Cantillo LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 87+1063+913, pitcher rows 753+1681+759

Verdict

Cantillo's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Jose Trevino should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.185 xwOBA (-130 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +27 pts (raw 0.193 -> adj 0.220); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -35 pts (bb_type-adj 0.220 -> final 0.185); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Joey Cantillo is in a 14-day hot stretch (-46 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

11.Hao-Yu LeeRHBDET vs Mason Fluharty (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.207 / xBA 0.151 / xSLG 0.348 (-108 pts)K 30.0%BB 5.5%HR 0.0%Hit 13.7%Strong Pitcher

Hao-Yu Lee RHB vs Mason Fluharty LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 197+0+0, pitcher rows 284+1017+0

Verdict

Fluharty's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Hao-Yu Lee should look to drive the changeup. Projection: 0.207 xwOBA (-108 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -10 pts (raw 0.217 -> adj 0.207); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

12.Spencer HorwitzLHBPIT vs Cristopher Sánchez (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.209 / xBA 0.217 / xSLG 0.206 (-106 pts)K 24.1%BB 6.6%HR 0.0%Hit 17.0%Strong Pitcher

Spencer Horwitz LHB vs Cristopher Sánchez LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 582+1699+1588, pitcher rows 762+3086+2886

Verdict

Sánchez's sinker is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.209 xwOBA (-106 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -15 pts (raw 0.253 -> adj 0.238); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -28 pts (bb_type-adj 0.238 -> final 0.209); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Cristopher Sánchez is in a 14-day hot stretch (-98 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

13.Spencer JonesLHBNYY vs Huascar Brazobán (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.210 / xBA 0.174 / xSLG 0.157 (-105 pts)K 24.9%BB 22.9%HR 0.0%Hit 6.7%Strong Pitcher

Spencer Jones LHB vs Huascar Brazobán RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 82+0+0, pitcher rows 325+1017+903

Verdict

Brazobán's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Spencer Jones should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.210 xwOBA (-105 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -23 pts (raw 0.234 -> adj 0.210); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Verdict

Sánchez's sinker is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.211 xwOBA (-104 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: +35 pts (bb_type-adj 0.176 -> final 0.211); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Cristopher Sánchez is in a 14-day hot stretch (-98 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 73.9 mph
swing length 7.5 ft
attack angle 7.4°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Sinker-6.6+2.451-4.2swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Slider-8.4+10.955+2.5on plane
Changeup-8.4+13.36+4.9swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 15.Riley GreeneLHBDET vs Mason Fluharty (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.213 / xBA 0.063 / xSLG 0.204 (-102 pts)K 41.4%BB 11.3%HR 1.8%Hit 9.9%Strong Pitcher

Riley Greene LHB vs Mason Fluharty LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 762+2826+2545, pitcher rows 284+1017+0

Verdict

Fluharty's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.213 xwOBA (-102 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: +37 pts (bb_type-adj 0.176 -> final 0.213); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Riley Greene is on a 14-day slump (-42 pts vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Sweeper54.70.2890.197GB 39% / LD 11% / FB 45% / PU 5%0.294+40.1760.0980.32855.9

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

16.Brandon LoweLHBPIT vs Cristopher Sánchez (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.214 / xBA 0.166 / xSLG 0.216 (-101 pts)K 35.2%BB 2.7%HR 2.4%Hit 15.9%Strong Pitcher

Brandon Lowe LHB vs Cristopher Sánchez LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 731+2148+1632, pitcher rows 762+3086+2886

Verdict

Sánchez's slider is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.214 xwOBA (-101 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -7 pts (raw 0.199 -> adj 0.192); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +23 pts (bb_type-adj 0.192 -> final 0.214); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Brandon Lowe is on a 14-day heater (+52 pts vs season). Cristopher Sánchez is in a 14-day hot stretch (-98 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

17.Mike YastrzemskiLHBATL vs Payton Tolle (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.218 / xBA 0.143 / xSLG 0.215 (-97 pts)K 25.7%BB 3.1%HR 0.3%Hit 12.6%Edge Pitcher

Mike Yastrzemski LHB vs Payton Tolle LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 541+2321+1960, pitcher rows 325+311+0

Verdict

Tolle's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Mike Yastrzemski should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.218 xwOBA (-97 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -9 pts (raw 0.216 -> adj 0.208); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.208 -> final 0.218); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Mike Yastrzemski is on a 14-day heater (+61 pts vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Ryan O'Hearn LHB vs Cristopher Sánchez LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 723+2240+1938, pitcher rows 762+3086+2886

Verdict

Sánchez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.220 xwOBA (-95 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -23 pts (raw 0.219 -> adj 0.196); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +23 pts (bb_type-adj 0.196 -> final 0.220); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Ryan O'Hearn is on a 14-day slump (-87 pts vs season). Cristopher Sánchez is in a 14-day hot stretch (-98 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

20.Freddy FerminRHBSD vs Logan Gilbert (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.224 / xBA 0.207 / xSLG 0.298 (-91 pts)K 22.0%BB 2.7%HR 1.0%Hit 19.3%Edge Pitcher

Freddy Fermin RHB vs Logan Gilbert RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 318+1420+1438, pitcher rows 733+2412+3043

Verdict

Gilbert's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Freddy Fermin should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.224 xwOBA (-91 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +11 pts (raw 0.210 -> adj 0.221); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Freddy Fermin is on a 14-day slump (-46 pts vs season). Logan Gilbert is in a 14-day rough patch (+71 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 66.7 mph
swing length 6.5 ft
attack angle 6.4°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-5.4-4.27-9.6swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Sinker-6.1-2.98-9.0swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Curveball-9.7+6.4*1-3.2swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Slider-8.0+11.48+3.4swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Cutter-6.6+6.4*0-0.1on plane
Changeup-7.7+6.4*1-1.2on plane

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

22.Zach DezenzoRHBHOU vs Jacob deGrom (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.226 / xBA 0.168 / xSLG 0.334 (-89 pts)K 38.2%BB 4.6%HR 1.2%Hit 17.5%Edge Pitcher

Zach Dezenzo RHB vs Jacob deGrom RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 88+480+245, pitcher rows 712+2617+172

Verdict

deGrom's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Zach Dezenzo should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.226 xwOBA (-89 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: +5 pts (bb_type-adj 0.220 -> final 0.226); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

23.Christian VázquezRHBHOU vs Jacob deGrom (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.226 / xBA 0.201 / xSLG 0.316 (-89 pts)K 21.0%BB 2.9%HR 2.4%Hit 18.7%Edge Pitcher

Christian Vázquez RHB vs Jacob deGrom RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 345+834+1141, pitcher rows 712+2617+172

Verdict

deGrom's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Christian Vázquez should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.226 xwOBA (-89 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +14 pts (raw 0.220 -> adj 0.234); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.234 -> final 0.226); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Christian Vázquez is on a 14-day slump (-51 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

24.Hyeseong KimLHBLAD vs José Soriano (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.227 / xBA 0.199 / xSLG 0.237 (-88 pts)K 24.1%BB 6.9%HR 0.9%Hit 18.5%Edge Pitcher

Hyeseong Kim LHB vs José Soriano RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 384+660+0, pitcher rows 764+2784+1743

Verdict

Soriano's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Hyeseong Kim should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.227 xwOBA (-88 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -31 pts (raw 0.235 -> adj 0.204); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +24 pts (bb_type-adj 0.204 -> final 0.227); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Hyeseong Kim is on a 14-day slump (-93 pts vs season). José Soriano is in a 14-day rough patch (+82 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

25.Nick AllenRHBHOU vs Jacob deGrom (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.228 / xBA 0.204 / xSLG 0.266 (-87 pts)K 28.8%BB 3.1%HR 0.0%Hit 22.1%Edge Pitcher

Nick Allen RHB vs Jacob deGrom RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 109+1583+356, pitcher rows 712+2617+172

Verdict

deGrom's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Nick Allen should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.228 xwOBA (-87 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +20 pts (raw 0.207 -> adj 0.226); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Sinker0.60.2890.530GB 35% / LD 27% / FB 31% / PU 7%0.318+290.5330.5200.7363.0

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 60.3 mph
swing length 6.1 ft
attack angle 4.7°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.4+2.484-2.0on plane
Slider-7.3+11.649+4.2swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Changeup-7.5+8.510+1.0on plane
Curveball-8.5+9.715+1.2on plane
Sinker-5.2-3.569-8.6swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

26.Zach McKinstryLHBDET vs Mason Fluharty (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.230 / xBA 0.169 / xSLG 0.229 (-85 pts)K 33.6%BB 9.4%HR 1.4%Hit 11.6%Edge Pitcher

Zach McKinstry LHB vs Mason Fluharty LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 306+2180+1278, pitcher rows 284+1017+0

Verdict

Fluharty's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Zach McKinstry should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.230 xwOBA (-85 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -9 pts (raw 0.246 -> adj 0.237); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.237 -> final 0.230); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

27.Brent RookerRHBATH vs Trevor McDonald (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.232 / xBA 0.209 / xSLG 0.262 (-83 pts)K 29.7%BB 2.3%HR 2.7%Hit 19.3%Edge Pitcher

Brent Rooker RHB vs Trevor McDonald RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 424+2590+2391, pitcher rows 182+213+39

Verdict

McDonald's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Brent Rooker should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.232 xwOBA (-83 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -71 pts (raw 0.282 -> adj 0.211); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +21 pts (bb_type-adj 0.211 -> final 0.232); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

28.Jacob YoungRHBWSH vs Chris Bassitt (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.233 / xBA 0.232 / xSLG 0.249 (-82 pts)K 19.1%BB 3.4%HR 0.7%Hit 18.3%Edge Pitcher

Jacob Young RHB vs Chris Bassitt RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 576+1368+1956, pitcher rows 703+2958+2976

Verdict

Bassitt's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Jacob Young should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.233 xwOBA (-82 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -11 pts (raw 0.242 -> adj 0.230); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. 29.Wenceel PérezLHBDET vs Mason Fluharty (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.237 / xBA 0.100 / xSLG 0.278 (-78 pts)K 30.6%BB 8.2%HR 5.7%Hit 10.5%Edge Pitcher

Wenceel Pérez LHB vs Mason Fluharty LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 337+1620+1829, pitcher rows 284+1017+0

Verdict

Fluharty's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Wenceel Pérez should look to drive the sweeper. Projection: 0.237 xwOBA (-78 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -21 pts (raw 0.252 -> adj 0.230); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.230 -> final 0.237); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Sweeper54.30.4480.197GB 39% / LD 11% / FB 45% / PU 5%0.445-30.3270.1380.51145.1
Cutter45.70.2030.271GB 37% / LD 23% / FB 17% / PU 23%0.160-430.1160.0560.00021.5

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Zach Cole LHB vs Jacob deGrom RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 122+224+0, pitcher rows 712+2617+172

Verdict

deGrom's changeup is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.240 xwOBA (-75 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -5 pts (raw 0.273 -> adj 0.267); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -27 pts (bb_type-adj 0.267 -> final 0.240); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

31.Dalton RushingLHBLAD vs José Soriano (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.243 / xBA 0.193 / xSLG 0.252 (-72 pts)K 33.4%BB 8.5%HR 0.6%Hit 17.5%Edge Pitcher

Dalton Rushing LHB vs José Soriano RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 270+604+0, pitcher rows 764+2784+1743

Verdict

Soriano's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Dalton Rushing should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.243 xwOBA (-72 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.237 -> adj 0.220); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +23 pts (bb_type-adj 0.220 -> final 0.243); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Dalton Rushing is on a 14-day slump (-172 pts vs season). José Soriano is in a 14-day rough patch (+82 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 71.2 mph
swing length 7.6 ft
attack angle 13.7°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Sinker-6.5+8.913+2.4on plane
Knuckle Curve-9.0+13.7*3+4.7swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

32.Rafael MarchánLHBPHI vs Bubba Chandler (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.243 / xBA 0.178 / xSLG 0.319 (-72 pts)K 16.2%BB 6.1%HR 0.5%Hit 19.9%Edge Pitcher

Rafael Marchán LHB vs Bubba Chandler RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 219+459+195, pitcher rows 679+461+0

Verdict

Chandler's changeup is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.243 xwOBA (-72 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +38 pts (raw 0.204 -> adj 0.242); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

34.Luis TorrensRHBNYM vs Carlos Rodón (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.245 / xBA 0.182 / xSLG 0.340 (-70 pts)K 22.1%BB 7.0%HR 0.1%Hit 17.7%Edge Pitcher

Luis Torrens RHB vs Carlos Rodón LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 223+1070+513, pitcher rows 78+3375+3415

Verdict

Rodón's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Luis Torrens should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.245 xwOBA (-70 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -47 pts (raw 0.287 -> adj 0.240); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

35.Austin HedgesRHBCLE vs Chris Paddack (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.246 / xBA 0.198 / xSLG 0.307 (-69 pts)K 23.5%BB 4.4%HR 1.6%Hit 20.4%Edge Pitcher

Verdict

Paddack's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Austin Hedges should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.246 xwOBA (-69 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +22 pts (raw 0.227 -> adj 0.248); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 65.8 mph
swing length 7.1 ft
attack angle 10.1°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.9+10.753+5.8swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

36.Royce LewisRHBMIN vs Logan Henderson (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.247 / xBA 0.197 / xSLG 0.302 (-68 pts)K 41.0%BB 3.8%HR 4.0%Hit 18.9%Edge Pitcher

Royce Lewis RHB vs Logan Henderson RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 432+1518+1242, pitcher rows 185+410+0

Verdict

Henderson's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Royce Lewis should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.247 xwOBA (-68 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +9 pts (raw 0.227 -> adj 0.236); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +11 pts (bb_type-adj 0.236 -> final 0.247); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

37.Mauricio DubónRHBATL vs Payton Tolle (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.247 / xBA 0.204 / xSLG 0.316 (-68 pts)K 18.6%BB 4.7%HR 0.5%Hit 22.0%Edge Pitcher

Mauricio Dubón RHB vs Payton Tolle LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 638+1346+1463, pitcher rows 325+311+0

Verdict

Tolle's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Mauricio Dubón should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.247 xwOBA (-68 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +7 pts (raw 0.247 -> adj 0.254); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.254 -> final 0.247); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 58.7 mph
swing length 6.2 ft
attack angle -1.5°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-5.2-4.819-10.0swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Slider-8.5+4.117-4.4swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Cutter-7.1-1.5*0-8.6swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk

Changeup-7.9-1.5*0-9.4swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

40.Yoán MoncadaRHBLAA vs Justin Wrobleski (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.250 / xBA 0.204 / xSLG 0.258 (-65 pts)K 30.4%BB 9.5%HR 0.0%Hit 18.2%Edge Pitcher

Yoán Moncada RHB vs Justin Wrobleski LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 511+1134+192, pitcher rows 618+1175+593

Verdict

Wrobleski's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Yoán Moncada should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.250 xwOBA (-65 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +7 pts (raw 0.266 -> adj 0.273); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -23 pts (bb_type-adj 0.273 -> final 0.250); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Verdict

Wrobleski's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Bryce Teodosio should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.250 xwOBA (-65 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +6 pts (raw 0.245 -> adj 0.252); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

43.Javier SanojaRHBMIA vs Nick Martinez (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.254 / xBA 0.267 / xSLG 0.349 (-61 pts)K 7.3%BB 3.1%HR 0.3%Hit 22.2%Edge Pitcher

Javier Sanoja RHB vs Nick Martinez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 330+1221+118, pitcher rows 669+2603+2126

Verdict

Martinez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Javier Sanoja should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.254 xwOBA (-61 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +15 pts (raw 0.243 -> adj 0.258); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

44.Tyler HeinemanLHBTOR vs Casey Mize (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.255 / xBA 0.187 / xSLG 0.312 (-60 pts)K 22.2%BB 4.4%HR 3.2%Hit 18.2%Edge Pitcher

Tyler Heineman LHB vs Casey Mize RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 221+643+62, pitcher rows 473+2473+1790

Verdict

Mize's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Tyler Heineman should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.255 xwOBA (-60 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +24 pts (raw 0.224 -> adj 0.248); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.248 -> final 0.255); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 359+915+1155, pitcher rows 865+2893+0

Verdict

Alcantara's changeup is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.256 xwOBA (-59 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +21 pts (raw 0.235 -> adj 0.256); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Sandy Alcantara is in a 14-day rough patch (+49 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

46.Bryan ReynoldsLHBPIT vs Cristopher Sánchez (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.257 / xBA 0.217 / xSLG 0.259 (-58 pts)K 29.9%BB 5.3%HR 1.0%Hit 22.1%Edge Pitcher

Bryan Reynolds LHB vs Cristopher Sánchez LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 780+2636+2616, pitcher rows 762+3086+2886

Verdict

Sánchez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Bryan Reynolds should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.257 xwOBA (-58 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -22 pts (raw 0.267 -> adj 0.245); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.245 -> final 0.257); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Cristopher Sánchez is in a 14-day hot stretch (-98 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Edouard Julien LHB vs Eduardo Rodriguez LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 544+876+1236, pitcher rows 756+2597+871

Verdict

Rodriguez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Edouard Julien should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.260 xwOBA (-55 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -54 pts (bb_type-adj 0.314 -> final 0.260); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Edouard Julien is on a 14-day slump (-91 pts vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

49.Luke KeaschallRHBMIN vs Logan Henderson (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.262 / xBA 0.239 / xSLG 0.325 (-53 pts)K 26.3%BB 6.5%HR 1.1%Hit 20.1%Edge Pitcher

Luke Keaschall RHB vs Logan Henderson RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 700+775+0, pitcher rows 185+410+0

Verdict

Henderson's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Luke Keaschall should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.262 xwOBA (-53 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.272 -> final 0.262); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 67.3 mph
swing length 7.0 ft
attack angle 9.4°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.0+5.0177+1.0on plane
Changeup-6.7+14.679+7.8swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Cutter-4.9+10.345+5.3swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Slider-7.5+12.176+4.6swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).
